introSection:
Sky Scream is a steel roller coaster located at Plopsaland Deutschland in Hassloch, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. It is a Sky Rocket II built by Premier Rides on the spot previously occupied by Super Wirbel.

The ride was constructed by RCS GmbH. The support structure was fabricated by Valley Iron.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The addition of the coaster was announced in August 2013.

The ride was manufactured for a Chinese park, but after suffering delays, it was sent to Holiday Park instead, who had also ordered a new roller coaster. It was manufactured in the United States, with most of the ride arriving at the park during January 2014.

On 5 March 2014, the train arrived on site. Sky Scream opened on 12 April 2014.

Upon opening, the ride had no theme. For the 2015 season, the buildings surrounding the ride were styled in a zombie theme. The ride's queue line was also themed with the addition of some spooky theming elements.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Red track and light grey supports.

sections.title: Theme
sections.content: The ride has a horror theme and the buildings around it are designed to look like a derelict industrial complex.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Riders are held in by lap-bars.

fullText:
Sky Scream is a steel roller coaster located at Plopsaland Deutschland in Hassloch, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany. It is a Sky Rocket II built by Premier Rides on the spot previously occupied by Super Wirbel.

The ride was constructed by RCS GmbH. The support structure was fabricated by Valley Iron.

The addition of the coaster was announced in August 2013.

The ride was manufactured for a Chinese park, but after suffering delays, it was sent to Holiday Park instead, who had also ordered a new roller coaster. It was manufactured in the United States, with most of the ride arriving at the park during January 2014.

On 5 March 2014, the train arrived on site. Sky Scream opened on 12 April 2014.

Upon opening, the ride had no theme. For the 2015 season, the buildings surrounding the ride were styled in a zombie theme. The ride's queue line was also themed with the addition of some spooky theming elements.

Red track and light grey supports.

The ride has a horror theme and the buildings around it are designed to look like a derelict industrial complex.

Riders are held in by lap-bars.
